channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: 89-year-old woman dies after vehicle crashes into Labrador home on Gold Coast
item.description: An 89-year-old woman has died following a serious crash in the Gold Coast suburb of Labrador, where a vehicle collided with a residential property. The incident occurred at approximately 3:40pm on Fri...

item.title: Restoring Kenya's forests for long-term economic and ecological benefits
item.description: The article explains how Kenya's forests are not only important for nature but also for the economy. It shows that when forests are destroyed or degraded, the country loses money through lost water ca...

item.title: Engineered Probiotic E. coli Bacteria Produce Anticancer Drug Romidepsin and Target Tumors in Mouse Models
item.description: Researchers from Shandong University in China have engineered the probiotic bacterium Escherichia coli Nissle 1917 (EcN) to function as a targeted drug delivery system for cancer treatment. By genetic...

item.title: Governor Soludo Says APGA Will Support Tinubu and Not Present Any Presidential Candidate in 2027
item.description: Anambra State Governor, Professor Charles Soludo, don talk say him party — the All Progressives Grand Alliance (APGA) — no go put any presidential candidate for the 2027 general elections. The governo...

item.title: China-linked vessels gain safer passage through the Strait of Hormuz amid Iran conflict
item.description: The Strait of Hormuz, one of the world’s most crucial maritime chokepoints, has become a symbol of geopolitical tension following the outbreak of conflict between Iran and the US-Israel alliance. As t...

item.title: Scotland's assisted dying debate overlooks deep inequalities in palliative care and dying in poverty
item.description: The Scottish Parliament rejected the Assisted Dying for Terminally Ill Adults Bill by twelve votes on 17 March 2026, following an emotionally charged debate focused largely on safeguards, eligibility,...

item.title: Government assures South Africans of stable fuel supply amid global oil price concerns
item.description: The Department of Mineral and Petroleum Resources, together with the Fuels Industry Association, has reassured South Africans that fuel supply remains stable despite recent spikes in global crude oil ...

item.title: President and PM Emphasise Muslim Unity and National Harmony on Eid
item.description: On the occasion of Eidul Fitr, both President Asif Ali Zardari and Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif appealed to the nation for greater unity and brotherhood among Muslims, highlighting the difficult time...

item.title: Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma and spouse report increase in family wealth to Rs 35 crore in 2026
item.description: The affidavit filed by Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma along with his nomination papers for the Jalukbari constituency in the upcoming Assembly elections reveals a significant increase in the...

item.title: Tanzania Calls on Chinese Companies and Experts for Technology Partnership and Industrial Growth
item.description: The Tanzanian government is actively seeking collaboration with Chinese companies and experts to boost modern technology and innovation in the country. This move aims to speed up Tanzania's industrial...

item.title: Government directs Chinese contractor CRJE to finish AFCON 2027 access roads in Arusha two months early
item.description: The Tanzanian government has instructed the Chinese contractor CRJE to complete all road infrastructure works leading to the new AFCON stadium in Arusha City two months before the start of the Africa ...

item.title: Dubai real estate market faces slowdown amid US-Israel-Iran conflict
item.description: Dubai's property market is showing initial signs of weakness nearly three weeks after the start of the US-Israeli war on Iran. According to Goldman Sachs analysts, real estate transaction volumes in t...

item.title: Reyneke reflects on Proteas Women’s performance despite Eden Park defeat
item.description: Rising Proteas Women star Kayla Reyneke shone despite South Africa’s six-wicket loss to New Zealand in the third T20I at Eden Park. Reyneke remained unbeaten on 34*, showcasing her growing influence i...

item.title: Kenya Government Pledges to Boost Flower Export Sector Through Stronger Industry Partnerships
item.description: The Kenyan government has renewed its commitment to working closely with industry players to overcome challenges in the floriculture sector, focusing on policy, regulation, and logistical hurdles. Thi...

item.title: B.C. Premier Considers Amendments to DRIPA Amid Court Rulings and Indigenous Opposition
item.description: British Columbia Premier David Eby is navigating complex pressures regarding proposed amendments to the Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples Act (DRIPA), a law that enshrines the United Nat...

item.title: Joe Rogan Compares Some U.S. Military Commanders' Religious Extremism to Suicide Bombers Amid Iran War Comments
item.description: Podcast host Joe Rogan, a 2024 Trump supporter, expressed strong criticism of certain U.S. military commanders during a recent episode of 'The Joe Rogan Experience' with guest Mark Norman. Rogan highl...

item.title: Philippine Coast Guard to Escort Fourth Civilian Mission to West Philippine Sea
item.description: The Philippine Coast Guard (PCG) is set to deploy a vessel to ensure the safety and security of the fourth 'Atin Ito' civilian mission to the West Philippine Sea (WPS). This deployment aims to provide...

item.title: How ageing affects opportunities for humour and laughter in older adults
item.description: A recent study explores the role of humour in the lives of people aged 60 and over, based on interviews with 20 participants who also rated their wellbeing. Contrary to assumptions that cognitive decl...

item.title: Guide to bringing your own containers for takeaway food in Australia
item.description: Australians generate millions of tonnes of plastic waste each year, much of it from single-use takeaway packaging like containers and bags. While reusable coffee cups have become commonplace, many sti...

item.title: Nowruz: The Iranian New Year and Its Cultural Significance Amid Conflict
item.description: Nowruz, meaning 'new day' in Persian, is the Iranian festival marking the start of the new year and the arrival of spring, tied to the spring equinox. Celebrated across Iran and other regions of Great...
